techniques.evidence_quote: large and well-known campaign to find and exploit open AWS S3 buckets on the Internet
Due to a misconfiguration in the S3 bucket that was hosting the library, a bad actor was able to inject code
someone using the TOR anonymizing network put a modified version of taskrouter.min.js into that path
A modified version of the TaskRouter JS SDK was uploaded to our site
a bad actor was able to inject code that made the user's browser load an extraneous URL that has been associated with the Magecart group of attacks
The script returned by the dot-tk URL redirects the user to some other sites, attempts to prevent the user from using their browser back button

attack_chain.description: Attacker opportunistically scanned the Internet for misconfigured/publicly writable AWS S3 buckets as part of a large-scale Magecart-associated campaign.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: this attack appears to be opportunistic and related to a large and well-known campaign to find and exploit open AWS S3 buckets on the Internet

evidence_chunks.quote: this attack appears to be opportunistic and related to a large and well-known campaign to find and exploit open AWS S3 buckets on the Internet
this attack appears to be opportunistic and related to a large and well-known campaign to find and exploit open AWS S3 buckets on the Internet

attack_chain.description: Attacker used the TOR anonymizing network to connect to the S3 bucket to obscure their origin.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: someone using the TOR anonymizing network put a modified version of taskrouter.min.js into that path

attack_chain.description: Attacker abused the S3 bucket's misconfigured policy (AllowPublicRead with s3:PutObject for Principal '*') to write to the taskrouter/* path.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: anybody on the Internet could read and write to that specific path

evidence_chunks.quote: anybody on the Internet could read and write to that specific path
anybody on the Internet could read and write to that specific path

attack_chain.description: Attacker uploaded a modified (trojanized) version of taskrouter.min.js v1.20 into the S3 bucket used to serve the Twilio-hosted JavaScript SDK from twiliocdn.com.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: A modified version of the TaskRouter JS SDK was uploaded to our site at 1:12 PM PDT

evidence_chunks.quote: A modified version of the TaskRouter JS SDK was uploaded to our site at 1:12 PM PDT
A modified version of the TaskRouter JS SDK was uploaded to our site at 1:12 PM PDT

attack_chain.description: The modified SDK was then distributed via Twilio's CloudFlare CDN and cached in user browsers, causing manipulated content to be delivered to downstream customers.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: The modified version may have been available on our CDN or cached by user browsers for up to 24 hours

evidence_chunks.quote: The modified version may have been available on our CDN or cached by user browsers for up to 24 hours
The modified version may have been available on our CDN or cached by user browsers for up to 24 hours

attack_chain.description: The injected JavaScript set a 'jqueryapi1oad' cookie, fetched a remote URL (gold.platinumus.top), and redirected victims' browsers (primarily mobile users) to malvertising/Magecart-associated sites, preventing back-button use and opening new targets transparently.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: The script returned by the dot-tk URL redirects the user to some other sites, attempts to prevent the user from using their browser back button, and opens new targets transparently

evidence_chunks.quote: The script returned by the dot-tk URL redirects the user to some other sites, attempts to prevent the user from using their browser back button, and opens new targets transparently
The script returned by the dot-tk URL redirects the user to some other sites, attempts to prevent the user from using their browser back button, and opens new targets transparently

initial_access_vector: Misconfigured AWS S3 bucket policy that granted s3:PutObject to Principal '*' (anyone on the Internet), allowing an anonymous attacker via TOR to overwrite a hosted JavaScript SDK file.
persistence_mechanism: None established; attacker relied on continued existence of the permissive S3 bucket policy. Modified JS remained cached on CDN/browsers for up to 24 hours post-remediation.
privilege_escalation: None required; bucket policy already allowed public write to the target path.
